purpose: Integrate governed-run with the runtime pipeline so a validated work order can flow from parsed plan → review → bounded execution under macOS sandbox-exec isolation → audit memory → manifest.
files_modified: intellagent_runtime/cli.py (extended governed-run: --execute, --output, mode resolution, mutual exclusion, wet-run flow via tools/os_isolation_runtime.execute_command_isolated, RefusalRecord sealing on every refusal/invalid/execution-failed path, manifest_hash, command_results)
Makefile (added governed-run-pipeline-check target; NOT in ci yet)
.gitignore (added /intellagent_refusals/ at the repo root; per-run refusal records are operational artifacts, not source)
files_NOT_changed_by_design: tools/** (no edits to existing pipeline_runtime / proposer_runtime / review_gate_runtime; the integration calls the lower-level os_isolation_runtime.execute_command_isolated primitive directly — see integration-decision below)

tests.macos_only_subset: 10 of the 40 are gated MACOS_ONLY because they invoke sandbox-exec; the other 30 run on all platforms and exercise dry-run, mode resolution, refusal sealing, manifest shape, audit chain, and CI/freeze invariants.

dry_run_result: PASS — backward-compatible with WO 015 behavior; manifest now also carries mode="dry-run", execution_plan_hash, and manifest_hash fields; existing dry-run tests continue to pass.
wet_run_result: PASS — wet-run flow exercised end-to-end on macOS via os_isolation_runtime.execute_command_isolated. A `pwd` work order produces GOVERNED_RUN_EXECUTED with all four pipeline-equivalent hashes (proposer/review/executor_manifest/pipeline) plus command_results.
refusal_result: PASS — every refusal path (invalid WO, refused plan, execution failure) seals a formal RefusalRecord via intellagent_runtime.refusal.RefusalStore.seal(), the canonical record hash is reported in the manifest as refusal_record_hash, and the file is materialized under <cwd>/intellagent_refusals/refusal-<id>.json.
audit_memory_result: PASS — audit chain stays AUDIT_CHAIN_VALID across dry-run and wet-run. Wet-run additionally appends governed_run.command.executed events per command and governed_run.completed at the end. audit_status is captured in the manifest.
refusal_record_result: PASS — formal RefusalRecord sealed on (a) invalid work order (parse error or file missing), (b) plan validation refusal, (c) execution failure. The intellagent_runtime.refusal.RefusalStore is reused unchanged.

integration_decisions.decision: Wet-run path: call tools/pipeline_runtime.run() (Workforce YAML) vs. call tools/os_isolation_runtime.execute_command_isolated() (sandbox primitive)?
integration_decisions.why: tools/pipeline_runtime.py is built for the Workforce YAML schema with fields (work_order_id, agent_role, allowed_files globs, required_gates, rollback_plan, human_approval_required, status_history, etc.) that do not map cleanly to a freeform markdown work order. Bridging would require inventing values for those required fields (synthetic status_history, heuristic rollback_plan, default human_approval_required) — exactly the kind of fake-it-to-make-it the standing law forbids. Additionally, pipeline_runtime.run() applies its OWN proposer→review filtering with a different admit-list logic from the WO-015 execution_plan validator, which would make the execution_plan validation vestigial. Calling execute_command_isolated() directly: (i) preserves the WO-015 validator as the authoritative gate; (ii) inherits sandbox-exec isolation, classifier deny-first, per-command timeout, and output cap; (iii) does not require an adapter that fabricates fields.
integration_decisions.what_would_change_my_mind: (a) A future work order that adds a faithful markdown→YAML adapter capable of populating every required Workforce schema field from the parsed WorkOrder without invention. (b) A pipeline_runtime variant that accepts a plan-as-data (rather than a YAML path) so the WO-015 plan can be passed directly. Either would let us route through pipeline_runtime.run() without lying about the format.
integration_decisions.cost_if_wrong: We lose one redundant pass of admit-list filtering and deterministic-hash checking that pipeline_runtime applies on top of the executor. The WO-015 plan validator + the os_isolation_runtime classifier remain in place as two layers. The third layer (pipeline_runtime's proposer/review) is forgone. Recoverable in a follow-up via the adapter mentioned above without re-doing the integration.

integration_decisions.decision: Proposer / review / executor hash provenance in the wet-run manifest.
integration_decisions.recommendation: Compute the four pipeline-equivalent hashes locally from the actual governed-run data: proposer_hash = sha256(canonical({planned_commands, work_order_hash})); review_hash = sha256(canonical({approved_commands, validation_status})); executor_manifest_hash = sha256(canonical(command_results)); pipeline_hash = sha256(canonical({proposer_hash, review_hash, executor_manifest_hash})).
integration_decisions.why: The manifest needs to be shape-comparable to a tools/pipeline_runtime manifest for any future reviewer or audit. Computing locally is honest accounting (the hashes commit to exactly what we did) and matches the structure pipeline_runtime would have emitted given the same inputs. No hash is fabricated; each one is derived from a real artifact in the run.
integration_decisions.what_would_change_my_mind: If pipeline_runtime.run() is integrated later via the YAML adapter, these fields would be replaced by the canonical pipeline_runtime hashes (proposer.deterministic_hash, review.review_hash, executor manifest sha256, pipeline_hash). The manifest field NAMES would stay; their derivation would change.
integration_decisions.cost_if_wrong: A reviewer comparing a governed-run manifest with a pipeline_runtime manifest would see different values for the same conceptual field. Documented here so the difference is visible.

known_limitations: Wet-run requires macOS — tools/os_isolation_runtime uses sandbox-exec. The 10 macOS-only tests are skipped on non-macOS. A Linux equivalent (namespaces / cgroups) is unimplemented in v0.1.
Wet-run does not pass commands through tools/pipeline_runtime — see Decision 1 above. The execution_plan validator + os_isolation_runtime classifier are the only filtering layers.

Per-command results capture status/exit_code/duration/profile hash. Full stdout/stderr capture is NOT included in the manifest (it is captured by the os_isolation_runtime per-run manifest but not echoed up). A reviewer can inspect reports/os_isolation_runtime/runs/*.json (when present) for the full output.
Concurrent governed-run invocations on the same audit file may interleave; audit_memory.verify_chain detects the resulting corruption but does not prevent it.

The workflow grammar parser still uses a keyword heuristic — WO prose containing words like "verify" or "execute" outside the workflow list can be misclassified as stages. Mitigation: every test fixture uses prose deliberately free of stage synonyms; real WOs that violate this surface the symptom as a no-verify / no-execute plan refusal.
RefusalRecord query field uses the work-order title or path; if both are empty the record carries a path-only query. This is acceptable because the rejected list always contains the reason strings.

risks_introduced.risk: Wet-run invokes real subprocesses under sandbox-exec.
risks_introduced.mitigation: Three independent gates: WO-015 execution_plan validator → os_isolation_runtime classifier deny-first → macOS sandbox-exec kernel profile. Output is capped, network is denied by default, fork is denied. Per-command timeout enforced.

risks_introduced.risk: Hash provenance for proposer/review/executor differs from tools/pipeline_runtime.
risks_introduced.mitigation: Documented in Decision 2; manifest names match pipeline_runtime; derivation is honest local computation.
/intellagent_refusals/ added to .gitignore at the repo root; per-run records remain on disk for audit but are not committed.

risks_introduced.risk: execute_command_isolated import depends on tools/ being on sys.path.
risks_introduced.mitigation: Lazy import; sys.path manipulation isolated to _execute_planned_commands; tested by all macOS-only tests.

next_build_task: Promote governed-run-check + governed-run-pipeline-check into make ci after one more cold-checkout confirmation run. The 5 WO-015 module self-check targets plus the WO-016 pipeline integration test would become CI stages 12–17 (after the existing 11). This converts governed-run from "opt-in surface" to a permanent CI gate.
